reportHeadline: Town battle back to claim a commanding win against
reportIntro: Bollington Town produced a gutsy, character-filled performance at Congleton High School, overcoming an early penalty setback to secure a dominant 4-2 victory over Denton Town — with Declan Hanks, Alfie Earith from the spot, Ally Harrison-Virani and Joshua Connolly all getting on the scoresheet in a memorable afternoon.

firstHalfEvents.title: Early Penalty Blow
firstHalfEvents.description: Town were dealt a cruel early setback when **Ryan Hibbert**'s mistimed shot inadvertently caught an opposition player, leaving the referee no choice but to point to the spot. Denton Town converted to take a shock lead inside the opening minute. 0–1.

firstHalfEvents.title: Ally Goes Close
firstHalfEvents.description: Town began to grow into the game when **Ally Harrison-Virani** drove a shot on target from 10 yards out on the left wing, only for the effort to clip a defender and be deflected wide. A sign of things to come from a lively Harrison-Virani.

firstHalfEvents.title: Eastham Effort Over
firstHalfEvents.description: **Jake Eastham** had a sight of goal and got a shot away, but the effort drifted over the crossbar. Town were pushing for an equaliser.

firstHalfEvents.title: Matt Denied by Keeper
firstHalfEvents.description: **Matthew Joyce** won a tense 1v1 in the box against Denton's number eleven, getting past his man, only for the opposition keeper to get down well and deny him. A chance that deserved better.

firstHalfEvents.title: Denton Miss a Sitter
firstHalfEvents.description: A chaotic scramble in the Town box should have put Denton further ahead, but an opposition player somehow missed from point-blank range with the goal gaping. A massive let-off — and Town made them pay within a minute.

firstHalfEvents.title: Goal — Declan Hanks
firstHalfEvents.description: Town drew level immediately. **Zach Gatward**'s inviting cross from the right found **Declan Hanks** arriving in the box, who finished with composure to restore parity. A richly deserved equaliser and a moment that changed the game. 1–1.

firstHalfEvents.title: Connolly Booked
firstHalfEvents.description: **Joshua Connolly** was shown a yellow card for a foul on the Denton goalkeeper — an unfortunate caution that didn't dampen his influence on the game as the half wore on.

firstHalfEvents.title: Late First Half Pressure
firstHalfEvents.description: Town finished the half on the front foot. **Declan Hanks** had a close chance at 43' that narrowly failed to find the target, **Connolly** tested the keeper from distance at 44', and **Ally Harrison-Virani** blazed an effort over in stoppage time. Both sides went in level, but Town looked the more dangerous.

secondHalfEvents.title: Goal — Alfie Earith (Pen)
secondHalfEvents.description: Town took the lead from the spot when a foul in the box handed them a penalty. **Alfie Earith** stepped up and converted with his usual composure, giving Bollington the lead they had been threatening for much of the match. 2–1.

secondHalfEvents.title: Goal — Ally Harrison-Virani
secondHalfEvents.description: Town doubled their advantage almost immediately, with a devastating one-two punch. **Zach Gatward**'s sharp pass slid **Ally Harrison-Virani** in behind the defence, and she finished clinically from seven yards out. A stunning blow for Denton. 3–1.

secondHalfEvents.title: Denton Pull One Back
secondHalfEvents.description: Denton Town refused to lie down and pulled a goal back within a minute to make it 3–2. A nervy moment for Town, but the response was emphatic.

secondHalfEvents.title: Connolly Offside
secondHalfEvents.description: **Joshua Connolly** had the ball in the net almost immediately after the restart, only for the flag to go up for offside. The goal was chalked off — but Connolly wasn't done.

secondHalfEvents.title: Goal — Joshua Connolly
secondHalfEvents.description: Connolly got his legitimate goal. **Zach Gatward** played a decisive through ball into the channel, and **Joshua Connolly** showed tremendous composure to hold off two centre-backs and finish clinically. The game was put to bed. 4–2.

secondHalfEvents.title: Zach Denied — Connolly Ruled Offside Again
secondHalfEvents.description: Town continued to press for more. **Zach Gatward** had a shot on target well saved by the Denton keeper at 63', before **Connolly** had another effort ruled out for offside at 64' — the flag going up for an offside pass from **Declan Hanks** that preceded the finish.

secondHalfEvents.title: Hanks Shot Saved
secondHalfEvents.description: **Alfie Earith**'s cross from the right found **Declan Hanks** in a dangerous area, but the Denton keeper produced a good save to deny him a brace. Town continued to create.

secondHalfEvents.title: Hibbert Booked for Dissent
secondHalfEvents.description: **Ryan Hibbert** picked up a yellow card for dissent after a disagreement with the referee. He then required a spell of injury treatment at 74' but battled on to see out his shift.

secondHalfEvents.title: Connolly Denied Again
secondHalfEvents.description: **Declan Hanks** whipped a cross into the box to find **Joshua Connolly**, whose shot on target was well saved by the Denton keeper. Town were relentless even with the points secured.

secondHalfEvents.title: Fresh Legs
secondHalfEvents.description: **Beau Gatward** came on to replace the excellent **Joshua Connolly**, who had given everything across his 82 minutes on the pitch.

secondHalfEvents.title: Zach's Final Fling
secondHalfEvents.description: **Zach Gatward** capped a superb personal display with a fierce shot in stoppage time that was somehow cleared off the line. A fitting end to a commanding team performance.

pullQuote: Zach pulled the strings all afternoon — two of those goals came directly off his pass, and he was unfortunate not to add his name to the scoresheet himself.
matchVerdict: Bollington Town showed tremendous character at Congleton High School, responding to a cruel opening-minute penalty with a performance full of quality and determination. The first half saw Town create chance after chance despite trailing — Joyce denied in a 1v1, Harrison-Virani going close, Hanks and Connolly both testing the keeper — before Zach Gatward's cross gave Declan Hanks the chance to level on 33'. Town went in at the break level and looking the stronger side.

The second half belonged to Bollington. Earith's penalty opened the floodgates, and Harrison-Virani's clinical finish moments later looked to have killed the game — before Denton pulled it back to 3–2 to set up a nervy finale. But Town showed real maturity. Connolly, twice denied by the flag, kept his focus and buried the fourth goal with composure under pressure — putting the result beyond any doubt.

**Zach Gatward** was the creative heartbeat throughout, providing two assists and going close himself on multiple occasions. **Joshua Connolly** was a constant thorn in Denton's side. A deserved, well-rounded win.
